message:
chore(dingtalk): document #[allow] justifications (br-c015t/pj20f/6lt8f/fc4uu/833aa)

Five undocumented #[allow(clippy::*)] in connectors/dingtalk/. All five
were verified necessary: removing each makes clippy fire (operations()
108 lines, invoke_inner 168 lines, handle_message 114 lines all exceed
too_many_lines threshold; operation() takes 8 args > too_many_arguments
default of 7; ParsedTarget::parse uses an if-let cascade that
option_if_let_else suggests rewriting). Added a short justification
above each so a future reader can decide whether the trade-off still
holds without running the lint to find out.

- connector.rs:124 operations() — 14 inline OperationInfo entries.
- connector.rs:234 invoke_inner() — single-match dispatch keeps the
  verify_bound + client-call shape adjacent for capability review.
- connector.rs:650 operation() — args mirror OperationInfo's own field
  count by design.
- main.rs:62 handle_message() — JSON-RPC dispatcher; per-method
  request/error/encode shape stays adjacent.
- types.rs:175 ParsedTarget::parse() — chat:/user: tags read better as
  an if-let cascade than an option_if_let_else map_or_else rewrite.

Verified: cargo clippy -p fcp-dingtalk --no-deps -- -D warnings clean.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
